chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-5563 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________________ FRESH CASE: 1. For order on CMA No.25735/2023 (Urgent). 2. For order on CMA No.25736/2023 (Exemption). 3. For order on CMA No.25737/2023 (Stay). 4. For hearing of main case. ----------- Dated; 16th November 2023 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, Advocate for Petitioner. -*-*-*-*-*- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 3&4. Through instant Constitutional Petition, the petitioner, who is a police constable (BPS-5) and allotted a quarter/flat No.5 in Block-5, Type-G situated at Napier Family Police Zone, Karachi vide Allotment Order dated 21.08.2023 pursuant to an order dated 28.09.2023 passed by the Divisional Bench of this Court in C.P. No.D-4414/2023, has expressed his grievance against inaction on the part of the respondents, who according to learned counsel for the petitioner, are not handing over the possession of the aforesaid quarter/flat inspite of the fact that respondent No.4, who was earlier allotted subject quarter/flat, had stood retired, was directed by this Court vide aforesaid order passed in the aforesaid petition to vacate such quarter/flat within a month from the date of such order, however, neither he is vacated the subject quarter/flat, nor official respondents are enforced the order passed by the Divisional Bench of this Court, or handing over the possession of subject quarter/flat to the petitioner. Let pre-admission notice be issued to the respondents as well as to the Advocate-General, Sindh, to be served through first three

No.D-4414/2023, has expressed his grievance against inaction on the part of the respondents, who according to learned counsel for the petitioner, are not handing over the possession of the aforesaid quarter/flat inspite of the fact that respondent No.4, who was earlier allotted subject quarter/flat, had stood retired, was directed by this Court vide aforesaid order passed in the aforesaid petition to vacate such quarter/flat within a month from the date of such order, however, neither he is vacated the subject quarter/flat, nor official respondents are enforced the order passed by the Divisional Bench of this Court, or handing over the possession of subject quarter/flat to the petitioner. Let pre-admission notice be issued to the respondents as well as to the Advocate-General, Sindh, to be served through first three [Page 2] modes, for 28.11.2023, when comments/reply, if any, shall be filed with advance copy to the learned counsel for petitioner. In the meanwhile, respondents shall ensure compliance of Court’s order, as referred to hereinabove, and if, there is no legal impediment subject quarter/flat may be handed over to the petitioner pursuant to Allotment Order dated 21.08.2023, preferably, within a period of fifteen days from the date of this order. ACTING CHIEF JUSTICE J U D G E *Farhan/PS*
